subject: Human Resources
title: AI in HR Functions
description: A micro-lesson that makes AI in HR Functions usable.

hook.text: An AI screens 10,000 resumes in seconds, ranks candidates, and schedules interviews—all before a human touches the process.

buildup.text: AI transforms HR—resume screening, chatbot onboarding, sentiment analysis, attrition prediction, and skills gap detection.

discovery.text: Predictive analytics can spot flight risks months before people resign—giving managers time to intervene and retain key talent.

twist.text: Amazon scrapped its AI hiring tool after it penalized resumes containing 'women's'—trained on biased historical data.

climax.text: AI amplifies whatever data it's trained on. Biased data creates biased AI. HR must audit algorithms, not just trust them.

punchline.text: AI makes HR faster. Humans make sure it stays fair.

quiz.question: Why did Amazon scrap its AI hiring tool?
options: It penalized resumes with the word 'women's' due to biased training data
It was too expensive to run
